---
title: Elements
---

Every rendered element (code blocks, figures, fenced divs, bracketed spans, theorems, callouts) passes through a template before becoming output. Element templates are small files that control the markup for a single component.


# How it works

A template is a file with {% raw %}`{{variable}}`{% endraw %} placeholders. For example, the built-in `div.html`:

```html
<div class="{{classes}}"{{id_attr}}>
{{children}}
</div>
```

Any {% raw %}`{{variable}}`{% endraw %} not defined is replaced with an empty string. Templates support the full [Jinja](jinja.html) syntax, including conditionals, loops, and filters.


# Naming and resolution

Templates are named `{element}.{format}` where format is `html`, `latex`, `typst`, or `markdown`:

- `callout-note.html`: HTML template for `::: {.callout-note}`
- `theorem.latex`: LaTeX template for `::: {.theorem}`
- `mystyle.html`: HTML template for `[text]{.mystyle}`

Resolution order:

1. Plugin `elements/` directory (in plugin order)
2. `templates/{target}/{name}.{ext}` (target-specific, e.g., `templates/website/callout.html`)
3. `templates/{engine}/{name}.{ext}` (engine-specific, e.g., `templates/html/callout.html`)
4. `templates/common/{name}.jinja` (format-agnostic)
5. Theme `templates/` directory (same three-layer resolution)
6. Built-in (compiled into the binary)

::: {.callout-tip}
Hyphens in class names are normalized to underscores when looking up templates. A div with class `.highlight-box` matches a template file named `highlight-box.html` or `highlight_box.html`.
:::


# Div variables

Fenced divs (`::: {.classname #my-id key="value"} ... :::`) receive:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{children}}`{% endraw %} | Recursively rendered content |
| {% raw %}`{{classes}}`{% endraw %} | Space-separated class list |
| {% raw %}`{{id}}`{% endraw %} | Element id (empty if none) |
| {% raw %}`{{id_attr}}`{% endraw %} | ` id="..."` attribute string (empty if none) |
| {% raw %}`{{label}}`{% endraw %} | Format-specific label: `\label{id}` (LaTeX), `<id>` (Typst), empty (HTML) |
| {% raw %}`{{format}}`{% endraw %} | Current output format |
| *user attrs* | Any `key="value"` pairs from the div header |

Template lookup tries each class in order, then falls back to `div.{format}`.


# Span variables

Bracketed spans (`[text]{.myclass key="val"}`) receive:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{content}}`{% endraw %} | Text inside the brackets (rendered as inline markdown) |
| {% raw %}`{{class}}`{% endraw %} | First class |
| {% raw %}`{{classes}}`{% endraw %} | Space-separated class list |
| {% raw %}`{{id}}`{% endraw %} / {% raw %}`{{id_attr}}`{% endraw %} | Same as divs |
| {% raw %}`{{format}}`{% endraw %} | Current output format |
| *user attrs* | Any key-value pairs |

If no template matches, *Calepin* falls back to `<span>` (HTML), `\text{}` (LaTeX), or `[]` (Typst).


# Code block variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{code}}`{% endraw %} | Raw source code |
| {% raw %}`{{lang}}`{% endraw %} | Language identifier |
| {% raw %}`{{label}}`{% endraw %} | Chunk label |
| {% raw %}`{{highlighted}}`{% endraw %} | Syntax-highlighted code (HTML/LaTeX/Typst) |

Related templates: `code_source`, `code_output` ({% raw %}`{{output}}`{% endraw %}), `code_warning`, `code_message`, `code_error`.


# Figure variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{image}}`{% endraw %} | Format-specific image tag |
| {% raw %}`{{path}}`{% endraw %} | Resolved image file path |
| {% raw %}`{{alt}}`{% endraw %} | Alt text |
| {% raw %}`{{caption}}`{% endraw %} | Figure caption |
| {% raw %}`{{label}}`{% endraw %} | Figure label |
| {% raw %}`{{number}}`{% endraw %} | Auto-incremented figure number |
| {% raw %}`{{width_attr}}`{% endraw %} / {% raw %}`{{height_attr}}`{% endraw %} | Format-specific dimension attributes |
| {% raw %}`{{align}}`{% endraw %} | Alignment: `left`, `center`, `right` |
| {% raw %}`{{cap_location}}`{% endraw %} | Caption position: `top`, `bottom`, `margin` |

See [Figures](../authoring/figures.html) for more details on figure rendering.


# Theorem variables

Theorem environments (`::: {.theorem}`, `::: {.lemma}`, `::: {.definition}`, etc.) receive additional variables from the theorem filter:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{type_name}}`{% endraw %} | Capitalized type ("Theorem", "Lemma", "Definition", ...) |
| {% raw %}`{{number}}`{% endraw %} | Auto-incremented counter per type |
| {% raw %}`{{type_class}}`{% endraw %} | Original class name |

Two built-in templates handle these:

- **Italic body** (theorem, lemma, corollary, proposition, conjecture)
- **Normal body** (definition, example, exercise, solution, remark, algorithm)


# Callout variables

Callout divs (`::: {.callout-note}`, `::: {.callout-warning}`, etc.) receive:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{callout_type}}`{% endraw %} | Type without prefix: `note`, `tip`, `warning`, `caution`, `important` |
| {% raw %}`{{title}}`{% endraw %} | Title text (default: capitalized type name) |
| {% raw %}`{{header}}`{% endraw %} | Icon + title combined |
| {% raw %}`{{collapse}}`{% endraw %} | `"true"` or `"false"` |
| {% raw %}`{{appearance}}`{% endraw %} | `"default"` or user-provided |


# Multi-format templates

Built-in templates handle all formats in a single file using conditionals:

```
{%- if format == "html" -%}
<div class="{{classes}}"{{id_attr}}>
{{children}}
</div>
{%- elif format == "latex" -%}
\begin{{{classes}}}
{{children}}
\end{{{classes}}}
{%- elif format == "typst" -%}
#block[
{{children}}
]
{%- endif -%}
```

User overrides are per-format (one file per format), so you only need to create the formats you want to customize.


# Custom template example

Create `templates/html/highlight-box.html` (or `_calepin/elements/highlight-box.html`):

```html
<div class="highlight-box" style="background: {{color}};
     border-left: 4px solid #333; padding: 0.8em 1em; margin: 1em 0;"{{id_attr}}>
<strong>{{title}}</strong>
{{children}}
</div>
```

Used as:

    ::: {.highlight-box title="Key takeaway" color="#e8f4f8"}
    Templates are the simplest way to customize *Calepin*.
    :::

::: {.highlight-box title="Key takeaway" color="#e8f4f8"}
Templates are the simplest way to customize *Calepin*. Drop a file in `templates/html/` (or `_calepin/elements/`) and any fenced div with that class will use it.
:::

::: {.highlight-box title="Different colors work too" color="#fef3c7"}
Each instance can pass different key-value attributes. Here `color` is yellow instead of blue.
:::


# Built-in element templates

*Calepin* ships with templates for all standard elements:

| Template | Used by |
|----------|---------|
| `div` | Generic fenced divs |
| `code_source` | Code chunks (source) |
| `code_output` | Code chunks (output) |
| `code_diagnostic` | Warnings, messages, errors |
| `figure` | Figures and images |
| `callout` | All callout types |
| `theorem_italic` | theorem, lemma, corollary, proposition, conjecture |
| `theorem_normal` | definition, example, exercise, solution, remark, algorithm |
| `proof` | Proof environments |
| `landscape` | Landscape-oriented sections |
| `title` | Document title |
| `subtitle` | Document subtitle |
| `authors` | Author metadata |
| `date` | Document date |
| `abstract` | Abstract text |
| `keywords` | Keywords list |
| `bibliography` | Bibliography section |
| `appendix` | Appendix wrapper |
| `preamble` | Format-specific preamble |

Override any of these by placing a file in `templates/{engine}/` (e.g., `templates/html/callout.html`) or `templates/common/` (e.g., `templates/common/callout.jinja`).
